text: A new family of ruthenium complexes of the general formula [Ruⁿ(tda)(py)₂]ᵐ⁺ (n=2–4) has been developed using the pentadentate ligand [2,2':6',2''-terpyridine]-6,6''-dicarboxylate (tda²⁻). Comprehensive characterization via X-ray diffraction, spectroscopy, and DFT calculations reveals that the coordination environment of the Ru center is highly dynamic and dependent on the oxidation state. While the Ru(II) species is six-coordinate with a dangling carboxylate group, the Ru(IV) species exhibits a rare seven-coordinate geometry. In aqueous solutions, these complexes undergo structural transformations to form "Ru-aqua" species. Specifically, [Ruᴵⱽ(OH)(tda-κ-N3O)(py)₂]⁺ (4H⁺) is identified as a remarkably efficient water oxidation catalyst. Electrochemical analysis using the foot of the wave method (FOWA) reveals record-breaking turnover frequencies (TOF) of 8,000 s⁻¹ at pH 7.0 and up to 50,000 s⁻¹ at pH 10.0, representing the highest rates reported to date for ruthenium-based catalysts under these conditions. DFT calculations elucidate the catalytic cycle, demonstrating that the dangling carboxylate group of the tda²⁻ ligand plays a critical functional role by facilitating intramolecular proton transfer during the O-O bond formation step. This internal base mechanism significantly lowers the activation free energy for water nucleophilic attack, providing a strategic blueprint for the design of high-performance molecular oxidation catalysts.
